questions.text: ................ is any combination of text, art, animation and video delivered to you by computer or other electronic.
questions.explanation: Multimedia is the integration of multiple forms of media (text, graphics, audio, video, etc.) delivered digitally.

questions.text: Animation is possible because of a biological phenomenon known as ............
questions.explanation: Persistence of vision is the optical illusion that occurs when visual perception of an object does not cease for some time after the light from it has ceased.

questions.text: When you allow an end user to control what and when the elements are delivered, what is it called?
questions.explanation: Interactive multimedia allows the user to control the flow and delivery of the content.

questions.text: ................ are used for headlines and bold statements.
questions.explanation: Sans serif fonts are often preferred for headlines and on-screen displays because of their clean appearance.

questions.text: To play rich media files created in Adobe's Flash or Apple's QuickTime, which of the following may need to be installed into the browser application?
questions.explanation: Plug-ins are software components that add specific abilities to a larger software application, like a browser.

questions.text: Multilayered DVD technology increases the capacity of current optical technology to ................
questions.explanation: A dual-sided, dual-layer DVD (DVD-18) can hold approximately 17 GB of data.

questions.text: The size of ................ images depends on the display resolution, and hardware and software capabilities
questions.explanation: Bitmaps (raster images) are resolution-dependent; their quality and file size are tied to the number of pixels (dots) in the image.

questions.text: In ................ color and intensity signals are mixed into a single carrier wave
questions.explanation: Composite video combines luminance (brightness) and chrominance (color) information into a single signal.

questions.text: ................ is concerned with the visual or aesthetic aspect of the project
questions.explanation: Animation focuses on the visual movement and aesthetic presentation in a multimedia project.

questions.text: Anti-aliased text must be:

choices.label: D
choices.text: Creates irregular boundaries in titles and headlines

questions.explanation: Anti-aliasing smooths the jagged edges (jaggies) of text by adding pixels of intermediate colors, creating soft boundaries.

questions.text: Bitmapped images:

choices.label: B
choices.text: Are not known as paint graphics

choices.label: C
choices.text: A data matrix describing the individual dots of an image

questions.explanation: A bitmap is a map of bits forming a grid of pixels (dots) to create an image.

questions.text: Composite video uses ........................... To connect camera to a TV or a monitor
questions.explanation: Composite video uses a single channel (one wire) to transmit all video information.

questions.text: A ................ Is a collection of characters of a single size and style belonging to a particular typeface family.
questions.explanation: Historically, a font was a set of characters in a specific typeface, style, and size.

questions.text: File compression is very important when preparing ................ files for the Web
questions.explanation: Compression reduces file size to improve load times and bandwidth efficiency for all media types.

questions.text: Light reflected from an object through the camera's lens is converted into electronic signal by:
questions.explanation: A Charge-Coupled Device (CCD) is a sensor used in cameras to convert light into electrical signals.

questions.text: The file size of a 5-second recording sampled at 20 kHz, 16-bit stereo (two tracks) would be about:
questions.explanation:
File Size = \( \text{Sampling Rate} \times \text{Resolution} \times \text{Channels} \times \text{Time} \) / 8. 
 \( (20,000 \times 16 \times 2 \times 5) / 8 = 400,000 \text{ bytes} = 400 \text{ KB} \).

questions.text: The American Standard code for Information Interchange (ASCII) is a:
questions.explanation: Standard ASCII uses 7 bits to represent characters (128 possible values).

questions.text: Digital audio is:
questions.explanation: Digital audio data (like WAV or MP3) is device independent; it contains actual sound data that sounds consistent across different playback hardware.

questions.text: Size of monophonic digital recording is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Always larger the size of stereo recording

choices.label: B
choices.text: Always double the size of stereo recording

choices.label: C
choices.text: Always less than the size of stereo recording

choices.label: D
choices.text: Always equal the size of stereo recording

questions.explanation: Monophonic recording uses one channel, while stereo uses two, making stereo files twice as large given the same specifications.

questions.text: ................ is the color system used to create color in printing
questions.explanation: Printing uses the subtractive color model (CMYK), where pigments subtract light wavelengths to create color.

questions.text: ................ refers to the number of the bits used to represent each sound sample.
questions.explanation: Sample size (or bit depth) defines the dynamic range of the audio by determining how many bits are used per sample.

questions.text: MIDI is:
questions.explanation: MIDI (Musical Instrument Digital Interface) is a protocol that provides instructions (notes, velocity, pitch) on how a sound should be played, rather than the digital recording of the sound itself. Therefore, it is not digitized sound.

questions.text: VR is:
questions.explanation: VR (Virtual Reality) is often considered an advanced extension of multimedia that provides an immersive, three-dimensional, computer-generated environment.

questions.text: Multimedia developers use which of the following to create products such as eLearning programs and mobile apps?
questions.explanation: Authoring tools are software used to integrate various multimedia elements (text, audio, video, graphics) into a single interactive application or program.

questions.text: ________________ places the highest performance demand on computer and its memory and storage.
questions.explanation: Video involves high-resolution graphics and high frame rates, which requires significant processing power, memory, and storage space compared to text or audio.

questions.text: ________________ is the color system used in monitors.
questions.explanation: Monitors use the RGB (Red, Green, Blue) color model, which is an additive color system where light is combined to create colors.

questions.text: A technique in which a series of progressively different graphics are used on each frame of movie film is called:
questions.explanation: Cel animation involves drawing every frame individually to create the illusion of movement.

questions.text: If the value of each sample in an audio file is rounded off to the nearest integer, then this process is called:
questions.explanation: Quantization is the process of mapping continuous values to a finite set of discrete levels, such as rounding analog levels to digital integers.

questions.text: On the Macintosh, digitized sounds may be stored as data files, resources, or applications such as:
questions.explanation: Legacy Macintosh sound formats were typically AIFF or SND resources. None of the specific options provided (WAN, MP3, MPT) are the unique resource-based storage types specifically being asked about in the context of legacy Macintosh system architecture.

questions.text: What is the approximate file size of a 10-minute Red Book Standard audio recording, in MB?
questions.explanation: Red Book standard is 44.1 kHz, 16-bit, stereo. Calculation: \(44,100 \times 2 \text{ bytes} \times 2 \text{ channels} \times 600 \text{ seconds} \approx 105.8 \text{ MB}\). 100 MB is the closest approximation.

questions.text: True or False: Plug-ins are used to play rich media files created in Adobe’s Flash or Apple’s QuickTime.
questions.explanation: Historically, browsers required specific plug-ins (like Flash Player or the QuickTime plugin) to play rich media content created by these technologies.

questions.text: The little decoration at the end of a letter stroke is known as a 'sans-serif'.
questions.explanation: False. The decoration is called a 'serif'. 'Sans-serif' literally means 'without serif'.

questions.text: In typography diagrams illustrating lowercase letters like 'p', the shadowed area representing the portion below the baseline is the:
questions.explanation: The portion of a letter that extends below the baseline (like the tail of 'p', 'q', or 'g') is called the descender, and that region is the descender area.

questions.text: Graphics used for saving photo-realistic images and detailed complex drawings are called:
questions.explanation: Bitmap (raster) graphics store information for every pixel, making them ideal for representing the complex color variations and details found in photographs.

questions.text: The color depth of an 8-bit image is:
questions.explanation: Color depth is calculated as \(2^n\) where n is the number of bits. \(2^8 = 256\) colors.

questions.text: True or False: Bitmaps can be converted to vector images.
questions.explanation: True. This process is called 'tracing' or 'vectorization', where software identifies paths and shapes within the bitmap to create a vector version.

questions.text: A full screen graphic resolution (640 x 480 pixels) at an 8-bit color will yield the following file size:
questions.explanation: Calculation: \(640 \times 480 \text{ pixels} \times 1 \text{ byte per pixel (8-bit)} = 307,200 \text{ bytes}\). Dividing by 1024 gives exactly 300 KB.

questions.text: Which of the following is the appropriate recording level (in dB) to get a high-quality clean digital recording?
questions.explanation: In digital audio, levels should peak below 0 dB to avoid clipping. A level around -3 to -6 dB provides a strong signal while leaving enough 'headroom' to prevent distortion.

questions.text: What is the approximate size of a recording with the following parameters in KB? Duration 10s, Sample rate 22.05 KHz, Bit rate 8, Type M (Mono).
questions.explanation: Calculation: \(22,050 \text{ Hz} \times 1 \text{ byte (8-bit)} \times 1 \text{ channel (Mono)} \times 10 \text{ seconds} = 220,500 \text{ bytes}\). Dividing by 1000 gives 220.5 KB.

questions.text: True or False: MIDI is device dependent.
questions.explanation: False. MIDI is a device-independent protocol; the file contains performance data that can be interpreted by any MIDI-compatible hardware or software.

questions.text: The most commonly used native image file format on Windows is:
questions.explanation: BMP (Bitmap) is the standard native image format for the Windows operating system.

questions.text: True or False: Vector images can be used for photorealistic images.
questions.explanation: False. While complex gradients can approximate realism, vectors are mathematical paths and are not suited for the pixel-by-pixel detail required for photorealism, for which bitmaps are used.

questions.text: A ________________ is a family of graphic characters, often with many type sizes and styles.
questions.explanation: A typeface is a set of characters of the same design (e.g., Arial or Times New Roman) that includes various styles and sizes.

questions.text: The number of bits used for the standard ASCII coding system is:
questions.explanation: Standard ASCII uses 7 bits to represent 128 characters. Extended ASCII uses 8 bits.

questions.text: CMY is a(n) ________________ color method used by printers.
questions.explanation: The CMY (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow) model is subtractive because the pigments absorb (subtract) light frequencies to produce color, which is the standard for physical printing.

questions.text: An eBook that does not offer the user navigational control, forcing the user to read from beginning to end, is an example of linear multimedia:
questions.explanation: Linear multimedia flows from beginning to end without user intervention or navigation control, much like a movie.

questions.text: What is the binary representation of this color: \( <\#F, \#0, \#0> \)?
questions.explanation: In 24-bit color, hex shorthand \#F00 (red) expands to \#FF0000. In binary, \#FF is 11111111, and \#00 is 00000000. Thus, the representation is 111111110000000000000000.

questions.text: What is the hexadecimal representation of this color: 101000101001000101000010?
questions.explanation: Dividing the binary into 8-bit chunks: 10100010 = \#A2, 10010001 = \#91, 01000010 = \#42. Note: The text in the image has a small typo compared to common byte groupings, but Option D matches the 24-bit conversion logic for the provided string.

questions.text: Which of the following is a cross-platform format?
questions.explanation: PNG (Portable Network Graphics) is a standard cross-platform web format. DIB is Windows-specific, and PICT is legacy Macintosh-specific.

questions.text: Which of the following is correct:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The larger the sample size, the worst the data described the recorded sound and the smaller the file size

choices.label: B
choices.text: The larger the sample size, the better the data described the recorded sound and the smaller the file size

choices.label: C
choices.text: The larger the sample size, the better the data described the recorded sound and the larger the file size

choices.label: D
choices.text: The smaller the sample size, the better the data described the recorded sound and the smaller the file size

questions.explanation: Increasing the sample size (bit depth) provides a more accurate representation of the sound (better quality) but results in more data being stored, increasing file size.

questions.text: Regarding waveforms:

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is impossible to reconstruct the original waveforms from a digitized one

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reconstruct 50% of original waveforms from a digitized one

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reconstruct 10% of original waveforms from a digitized one

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reconstruct 100% of original waveforms from a digitized one

questions.explanation: According to the Nyquist-Shannon sampling theorem, if a signal is sampled at a rate higher than twice its highest frequency, the original continuous-time signal can be perfectly reconstructed from the discrete samples.

questions.text: Sequencing software:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Places audio clips in order in a sound track

choices.label: B
choices.text: Applies filters to digital audio in a predetermined order

questions.explanation: In multimedia, sequencing software is primarily used to record, edit, and play back MIDI (Musical Instrument Digital Interface) data.

questions.text: The process of playing a sound file while part of the file is still downloading is called.........
questions.explanation: Streaming allows the user to begin playback before the entire file has been transmitted.

questions.text: What is the size of a bitmap image given an image resolution of \( 50 \times 20 \) pixels and a color depth of 16 bits?
questions.explanation: Total pixels = \( 50 \times 20 = 1000 \). Bits = \( 1000 \times 16 = 16,000 \) bits. Bytes = \( 16,000 / 8 = 2000 \) Bytes.

questions.text: Serif fonts are highly suggested to be used for headlines and bold statements.
questions.explanation: Sans-serif fonts are typically preferred for headlines because of their clean look, while Serif fonts are often used for printed body text to aid readability.

questions.text: Panoramic images are created by stitching together a sequence of photos around a circle and adjusting them into a multiple seamless bitmaps
questions.explanation: Stitching multiple overlapping photos into a single seamless image is the standard method for creating panoramas.

questions.text: The RGB model is a 24-bit methodology where color is specified in terms of red, green, and blue values ranging from 0 to 256
questions.explanation: In an 8-bit per channel RGB model (24-bit total), values range from 0 to 255 (256 distinct values). Range '0 to 256' implies 257 values.

questions.text: Which of the following is a color specification format?
questions.explanation: HBS (likely a typo for HSB - Hue Saturation Brightness) is a color specification model. GIF and TIFF are file formats.

questions.text: Before creating and designing an image or artwork in Multimedia, one should:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Plan their approach using flow charts and storyboards

choices.label: B
choices.text: Have multiple monitors, if possible, for lots of screen real estate.

questions.explanation: Effective design requires planning (storyboards), efficient workspace setup (monitors), and organization of software/assets.

questions.text: The design and labeling of the buttons should be an easy and fast task
questions.explanation: Interface design, specifically button logic and labeling, is often a difficult and time-consuming task to ensure user clarity and consistency.

questions.text: The reference from one document to another document, image, sound, or file on the Web is a (n):
questions.explanation: A link (hyperlink) connects one resource to another on the web.

questions.text: Which one of the following is/are NOT typically part of a multimedia specification?
questions.explanation: Multimedia specifications typically define assets like Sound, Text, Video, and Images. 'radio' (likely referring to the UI element or broadcast) is not a base content type.

questions.text: Which of the following is likely considered a typical method for word searching in hypermedia systems:
questions.explanation: Truncation involves using a wildcard (like *) to search for variations of a word root (e.g., 'comput*' for computer, computing, etc.).

questions.text: Character metrics such as 'points' is a general measurement applied to individual characters?
questions.explanation: Points are a standard unit of measurement for font size, describing the height of the character block.

questions.text: Text is NOT typically part of a multimedia specification.
questions.explanation: Text is one of the fundamental building blocks of multimedia.

questions.text: What is the size of a bitmap image (in KB) given an image resolution of \( 50 \times 20 \) pixels and a color depth of 60 bits?
questions.explanation:
Total bits = \( 50 \times 20 \times 60 = 60,000 \) bits. 
Bytes = \( 60,000 / 8 = 7500 \) Bytes. 
KB = \( 7500 / 1024 \approx 7.32 \) KB.

questions.text: The multimedia project consists of the messages and the content only.
questions.explanation: A multimedia project also consists of interactivity, logical structure, interface design, and delivery systems.

questions.text: Which term describes the process when the computer finally uses intricate algorithms to apply the effects you have specified on the objects you have created for a final 3D image?
questions.explanation: Rendering is the final step in creating a 3D image where the computer calculates light, shadows, and textures to produce the output image.

questions.text: Which of these is a reason why animation is perceived as motion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Our mind tries to read images by completing perceived actions.

choices.label: B
choices.text: An image remains in the eye chemically for a brief time after viewing

choices.label: C
choices.text: A sequence of videos is read as motion.

choices.label: D
choices.text: All of the above are valid reasons.

questions.explanation: Motion perception in animation relies on both biological factors (persistence of vision) and cognitive factors (phi phenomenon/interpolation).

questions.text: At one time, the technology that brought the greatest amount of multimedia to the classroom was the:
questions.explanation: Laserdiscs were the first major high-capacity optical storage medium used to bring high-quality video and interactive multimedia into classrooms before CD-ROMs became ubiquitous.

questions.text: The video technique that allows you to choose a color or range of colors that become transparent, allowing the video image to be visible behind those colors in the overlying image, is known as:

choices.label: E
choices.text: All of the above are appropriate names.

questions.explanation: Chroma key (often using green or blue screens) allows a specific color range to be replaced with another video or image source.

questions.text: Removing blank space or dead air at the beginning or end of a recording is sometimes called:
questions.explanation: Trimming is the process of removing unwanted portions of audio, typically the silence at the start or end of a clip.

questions.text: A project allows users to sit back and watch it just as they watch a movie or the television.
questions.explanation: Linear multimedia is characterized by a lack of user control over the sequence of presentation, similar to a traditional movie or TV show.

questions.text: In text blocks, adjust the leading for the most pleasing line spacing. Lines that are tightly packed are difficult to read.
questions.explanation: Leading is the vertical space between lines of text; insufficient leading (tightly packed lines) makes reading difficult.

questions.text: In \( 2-1/2D \) space an illusion of depth is created through:
questions.explanation: \( 2-1/2D \) techniques use visual cues like shadows, layering (height/z-index), and perspective (forced space) to simulate depth in a 2D environment.

questions.text: Vector-drawn images are used in one of the following

choices.label: B
choices.text: Applications that don't require the drawing of graphic shapes

choices.label: C
choices.text: Applications requiring drawing of complex objects

choices.label: D
choices.text: Graphic artists designing for the print media

questions.explanation: Vector images are ideal for drawing complex, scalable objects that require mathematical precision, such as CAD drawings or architectural designs.

questions.text: To develop a multimedia project only creative, technical, and organizational skills are required.
questions.explanation: Multimedia production also requires business, management, marketing, and legal skills beyond just creative and technical ones.

questions.text: Dithering is a process where by the color value of each pixel is changed to the closest matching color value in the target palette
questions.explanation: Dithering is the use of patterns or noise to simulate colors not in the palette. Simply changing a pixel to the closest match is called nearest-neighbor quantization.

questions.text: Computes the bounds of the shapes within a bitmap image and then derives the geometric object that describes that image. This process is called:
questions.explanation: Auto tracing is the process of converting a bitmap (raster) image into vector objects by calculating the paths of the shapes within it.

questions.text: The 3D process of extending a plane surface some distance, either perpendicular to the shape's outline or along a defined path, is called:
questions.explanation: Extrusion is a common 3D modeling technique that pushes a 2D shape into the third dimension to create volume.

questions.text: Which of the following provides a system for dynamically displaying a font?
questions.explanation: PostScript is a page description language and font technology used to define fonts and graphics for high-quality display and printing.

questions.text: MIDI sound files are suitable for the web because

choices.label: B
choices.text: MIDI files embedded in web pages load and play promptly

choices.label: C
choices.text: The length of a MIDI file can NOT be changed without affecting the pitch

questions.explanation: MIDI files are very small as they contain instructions, not audio data, making them fast to load. Although technical aspects of (C) vary by software, it is often grouped as an advantage/characteristic in basic multimedia contexts.

questions.text: The slower a user's connection, the longer he or she must wait for enough of the sound to download so that the entire file will have downloaded by the time the sound reaches the end. This effect is called:
questions.explanation: Streaming latency refers to the delay before playback starts, caused by the time needed to buffer enough data to ensure smooth playback relative to the connection speed.

questions.text: If a DHTML document includes a font face that is NOT installed on the user's computer, a browser will:

choices.label: B
choices.text: Use the operating system's default typeface.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Leave a blank space where that text is.

questions.explanation: Standard browser behavior is to substitute an unavailable font with a generic family or the system default font.

questions.text: A video signal transmitted with all the signals mixed together and carried on a single cable is known as:
questions.explanation: Composite video combines luminance and chrominance information into a single signal carried over one wire.

questions.text: Serif fonts are highly suggested to be used for headlines and bold statements.
questions.explanation: Serif fonts are generally preferred for body text in printed materials because the serifs help the eye follow the line. Sans-serif fonts are typically suggested for headlines, bold statements, and digital screens due to their clean appearance and better legibility at lower resolutions.

questions.text: Panoramic images are created by stitching together a sequence of photos around a circle and adjusting them into a multiple seamless bitmaps.

choices.label: D
choices.text: False, they are adjusted into a single seamless bitmap

questions.explanation: Panoramic stitching processes multiple photos and combines them into a **single** seamless bitmap image, not multiple ones.

questions.text: The RGB model is a 24-bit methodology where color is specified in terms of red, green, and blue values ranging from 0 to 256.
questions.explanation: In an 8-bit per channel (24-bit total) RGB model, there are 256 possible values per channel, which means the values range from **0 to 255**. Including 256 would represent a 257th value.

questions.text: The design and labeling of the buttons should be an easy and fast task.

choices.label: D
choices.text: False, it requires careful user experience (UX) consideration

questions.explanation: Interface design, including button placement and labeling, is a critical part of user experience (UX). It requires careful thought, consistency, and testing, so it is rarely a "fast" or trivial task.

questions.text: The size of digital audio can be reduced by __________ or by decreasing the sampling rate.
questions.explanation: The file size of digital audio is determined by the sampling rate, the bit depth (resolution), and the number of channels. Reducing any of these will reduce the overall file size.

questions.text: Character metrics such as 'points' is a general measurement applied to individual characters?

choices.label: D
choices.text: True, but only for bitmap fonts

questions.explanation: Points are a standard unit of measurement used in typography to define the size of characters and font metrics.

questions.text: Text is NOT typically part of a multimedia specification.

choices.label: D
choices.text: False, it is a primary component

questions.explanation: Text is a fundamental and almost universal component of multimedia specifications, providing context, labels, and content.

questions.text: The multimedia project consists of the messages and the content only.

choices.label: D
choices.text: False, it also includes the delivery method and interface

questions.explanation: A multimedia project involves not just content, but also the software, interactivity, user interface, and the hardware/medium used for delivery.

questions.text: Which process occurs when the computer finally uses intricate algorithms to apply the effects you have specified on the objects you have created for a final 3D image?
questions.explanation: **Rendering** is the process of generating a final 2D image or animation from a 3D model using computer software, calculating lighting, shadows, and textures.

questions.text: The American Standard Code for Information Interchange (ASCII) is a one byte coding system.

choices.label: D
choices.text: True, but only for extended ASCII

questions.explanation: Standard ASCII is 7 bits, but it is typically stored and handled as an 8-bit (one byte) unit in computer systems. Extended ASCII sets specifically use the full 8 bits.

questions.text: In text blocks, adjust the leading for the most pleasing line spacing. Lines that are tightly packed are difficult to read.

choices.label: C
choices.text: True, but only for small fonts

choices.label: D
choices.text: False, leading refers to character spacing

questions.explanation: Leading is the vertical space between lines of text. Proper leading is essential for readability; if lines are too close, it becomes difficult for the eye to track from one line to the next.

questions.text: To develop a multimedia project only creative, technical, and organizational skills are required.

choices.label: D
choices.text: False, it also requires business and legal skills

questions.explanation: Developing a professional multimedia project also requires business skills (for budgeting and marketing) and legal skills (for copyrights and licensing).

questions.text: Dithering is a process where by the color value of each pixel is changed to the closest matching color value in the target palette.

choices.label: C
choices.text: True, it is used for compression

questions.explanation: Dithering uses patterns of pixels of different colors to simulate a color that is not in the palette. Simply changing a pixel to the closest matching color is called nearest-neighbor quantization, which often leads to banding.

questions.text: The process of computing the bounds of the shapes within a bitmap image and then deriving the geometric object that describes that image is called:
questions.explanation: **Autotracing** (or vectorization) is the software process of converting a bitmap image into a vector image by identifying shapes and paths.

questions.text: A characteristic of old media is that they are transient in nature.
questions.explanation: Old media, such as print and film, are typically recorded on physical media and are persistent. In contrast, broadcast media (like live radio/TV) can be transient, but the term 'old media' generally encompasses permanent analog recordings.

questions.text: Like binary code, Morse code uses bits and bytes to represent the characters and symbols of human language.
questions.explanation: Morse code uses sequences of short and long pulses (dots and dashes), not the 8-bit byte structure used in computer binary systems.

questions.text: Regarding message content, the producer is likely to have more experience than the client.
questions.explanation: In multimedia production, the client is usually the subject matter expert (SME) regarding the specific content, while the producer is the expert in the production process and media technology.

questions.text: According to the principle of cultural transcoding, new or emerging media have two competing parts, the "cultural layer" and the "computer layer."
questions.explanation: Cultural transcoding is a concept by Lev Manovich describing how computerization turns culture into computer data, resulting in two layers: the cultural layer (human-perceived) and the computer layer (data structure).

questions.text: In the three Ps model, production includes editing and distribution of content.
questions.explanation: The three Ps are Pre-production, Production, and Post-production. Editing occurs in the post-production phase, and distribution follows post-production.

questions.text: _______ is a broad term used to describe the collection of apps and digital platforms used to facilitate electronic communication and sharing of "ideas, personal messages, and content through online communities."
questions.explanation: Social media is specifically defined by the facilitation of online communities and content sharing among users.

questions.text: The _______ represents the top-level of the hierarchical structure of a computer file system used for organizing folders and files.
questions.explanation: The root directory is the first or top-most directory in a hierarchy.

questions.text: Historically, legacy, or old media relied on _______ methods of recording and distribution.
questions.explanation: Legacy or old media (like vinyl records, VHS, and physical print) utilize analog recording methods, whereas new media is digital.

questions.text: Digital storage media that retain data after the computer is turned off are said to be _______
questions.explanation: Non-volatile memory is computer memory that can retrieve stored information even after having been power cycled (turned off and back on).

questions.text: A _______ is an organized series of boxlike frames and/or text used for previsualizing portions of a video or film project.
questions.explanation: A storyboard is a graphic organizer that consists of illustrations or images displayed in sequence for the purpose of pre-visualizing a motion picture, animation, or interactive media sequence.

questions.text: The _______ is the Hollywood studio format used in the production of motion picture films and scripted television comedy and drama programs.
questions.explanation: The screenplay is the specific industry format used for scripts in film and television production.

questions.text: A _______ is a full-color rendering and illustration of what an actual webpage or interface will look like in its final form, including sample text, images, graphics, and color.
questions.explanation: A mockup is a static design of a web page or application that includes many of its final design elements but is not functional, unlike a prototype.

questions.text: Super Media is................. media
questions.explanation: In multimedia theory, 'Supermedia' is often used to describe the convergence and dominance of mass media systems in the digital age.

questions.text: Which of the following has contributed significantly to the development and spread of multimedia?
questions.explanation: The Graphical User Interface (GUI) was a fundamental advancement that allowed non-technical users to interact with multimedia content easily, leading to its widespread adoption.

questions.text: Multimedia producers and designers rely on data about consumers' demographics, such as:
questions.explanation: Demographics include statistical data like age and gender. Opinions and attitudes are classified as psychographics. Note: In the source exam, this is a multiple-answer question where both A and D are correct.

questions.text: A full-screen graphic resolution \( 640 \times 480 \) pixels at a 16-bit color will yield the following file size in KB:
questions.explanation: Calculation: \( 640 \times 480 \times 16 \text{ bits} = 4,915,200 \text{ bits} \). Convert to bytes: \( 4,915,200 / 8 = 614,400 \text{ bytes} \). Convert to KB: \( 614,400 / 1024 = 600 \text{ KB} \).

questions.text: What is the file size of a 10-second sound recorded using CD-ROM/XA format at a standard data rate of 150 KB/s?
questions.explanation: Using the base CD-ROM data rate (1x) of 150 KB/s: \( 10 \text{ s} \times 150 \text{ KB/s} = 1500 \text{ KB} \). Converting to binary MB: \( 1500 / 1024 \approx 1.46 \text{ MB} \).

questions.text: What is the file size of 15 minutes video in \( 6000 \times 4000 \) screen resolution with 24-bit depth (assuming 30 fps uncompressed)?
questions.explanation: Calculation: \( 6000 \times 4000 = 24,000,000 \text{ pixels/frame} \). \( 24,000,000 \times 3 \text{ bytes (24-bit)} = 72,000,000 \text{ bytes/frame} \). \( 72,000,000 \times 30 \text{ fps} = 2,160,000,000 \text{ bytes/sec} \). For 15 minutes (900 seconds): \( 2,160,000,000 \times 900 = 1,944,000,000,000 \text{ bytes} \approx 1,944 \text{ GB} \).

questions.text: What is the file size of a 20-second sound recorded using a sample rate of 22.05 kHz and a bit depth of 16 in a monophonic type in MB?
questions.explanation: Calculation: \( 22,050 \text{ samples/sec} \times 2 \text{ bytes (16-bit)} \times 1 \text{ channel} = 44,100 \text{ bytes/sec} \). For 20 seconds: \( 44,100 \times 20 = 882,000 \text{ bytes} \). Convert to binary MB: \( 882,000 / (1024 \times 1024) \approx 0.84 \text{ MB} \).
